title: Cloudflare Ships Agent Skills for Zero Trust Deployment and Migration
gist: Cloudflare released the Cloudflare One open-source library of AI agent skills that enable planning, deploying, managing, and migrating Zero Trust network security environments without requiring practitioners to learn the full product suite. The migration capability includes explicit logic for moving from Zscaler and Palo Alto Networks to Cloudflare, using the same logic that already moved enterprise customers in hours rather than months, making it self-serve for any customer or partner.

themes.theme: AI Agents for Zero Trust Security Automation

insights.claim: Cloudflare released the Cloudflare One stack, an open-source library of AI agent skills that give agents the knowledge to plan, deploy, manage, and migrate Zero Trust environments from Zscaler and Palo Alto Networks to Cloudflare without practitioners needing to learn the full product suite.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: Cloudflare is using AI agents as a competitive displacement tool against Zscaler and Palo Alto — reducing migration friction is a strategic move to capture enterprise Zero Trust market share.

insights.claim: The Cloudflare One migration skill contains the same logic used in Cloudflare's Descaler and Deskope programs, which have moved enterprise customers from Zscaler and Netskope to Cloudflare in hours rather than months, now made self-serve to any customer or partner without scheduling an engagement.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: Cloudflare is democratizing its most effective competitive sales tool — what previously required an expensive professional services engagement is now freely available, accelerating competitive displacement of Zscaler and Netskope.
insights.quote: The migration logic is the same logic used in Cloudflare's Descaler and Deskope programs, which have moved enterprise customers from Zscaler and Netskope to Cloudflare in hours rather than months.

insights.claim: The Cloudflare One agent skill covers the full VPN replacement lifecycle including Cloudflare Access user/network security, Gateway, Tunnel, Mesh, WAN connectivity, and Digital Experience Monitoring troubleshooting, generating deployment sequences that minimize disruption during cutover.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: End-to-end automation of VPN-to-SASE migration reduces the professional services barrier to adopting Cloudflare One — a structural threat to competitors' incumbent advantage from switching costs.

insights.claim: When paired with the Cloudflare MCP server, the agent skills gain a typed interface to the Cloudflare API that keeps authentication credentials out of the model context by handling them separately through the MCP server.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: Credential isolation from model context is becoming a security baseline for AI agents accessing infrastructure APIs; MCP as a security boundary layer for API credentials is an emerging architecture pattern.

insights.claim: The Cloudflare One stack uses a review-before-apply pattern where agents propose changes and generate summaries, but practitioners must review and approve before changes are committed, explicitly addressing enterprise concern about AI-generated modifications to security infrastructure.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: Human-in-the-loop approval gates are a prerequisite for enterprise adoption of AI agents in security configurations — tools without this pattern face policy barriers at security-conscious organizations.
insights.quote: Not replacing the security team, but compressing the route from intent to configured policy.

insights.claim: Cloudflare's agent skills ship as two lightweight files that can be loaded by any AI agent — cloudflare-one for product guidance and cloudflare-one-migration for vendor translation — making the capabilities framework-agnostic and available to the broader AI agent ecosystem.
insights.speaker: Steef-Jan Wiggers
insights.so_what: Open, file-based agent skill distribution allows Cloudflare to embed its product expertise into any agent framework — a distribution strategy that bypasses the need for bespoke integrations.
